channel.title: Toronto Seminars and Talks. Seminar Schools.
channel.description: An aggregated calendar of upcoming public lectures, seminars, author readings, artist talks, panel discussions, philosophy cafes, gatherings, and cinema events with director Q and A across the Greater Toronto Area. Sources verified for substantive content. Events that do not meet the four-condition test are excluded.

image.title: Toronto Seminars and Talks. Seminar Schools.

item.title: Rebecca Comay on Mourning Sickness
item.description: Lecture at University of Toronto Department of Comparative Literature. With Rebecca Comay. Hand-curated lecture. Comay on melancholia, mourning, and the dialectic of grief as developed in Mourning Sickness: Hegel and the French Revolution.

item.title: TPL Salon Series at Appel Salon
item.description: Lecture at Toronto Reference Library, Appel Salon, 789 Yonge Street. Toronto Public Library Salon Series.

item.title: Massey Dialogues
item.description: Lecture at Massey College, 4 Devonshire Place, Toronto. Massey College Dialogues series.

item.title: Cheryl Misak on Frank Ramsey
item.description: Lecture at University of Toronto Department of Philosophy. With Cheryl Misak. Hand-curated lecture. Misak on Frank Ramsey, the Cambridge philosopher whose biography she wrote (Frank Ramsey: A Sheer Excess of Powers, Oxford 2020).

item.title: Ian Hacking Memorial Symposium
item.description: Panel at Victoria College, University of Toronto. Memorial symposium for Ian Hacking, the late philosopher of science.

item.title: ROM Speaks on archaeology and memory
item.description: Lecture at Royal Ontario Museum, 100 Queens Park, Toronto. ROM Speaks public lecture series, theme: archaeology and memory.

item.title: Ethics@Noon-ish weekly talk
item.description: Lecture at Centre for Ethics, University of Toronto, Larkin Building, 15 Devonshire Place. Weekly Ethics@Noon-ish series. The Centre for Ethics workhorse seminar.

item.title: Jackman Humanities Annual Lecture
item.description: Lecture at Jackman Humanities Institute, 170 St. George Street, Toronto. Annual lecture of the Jackman Humanities Institute.

item.title: Sulpicia: Life, Love, and Literature in Ancient Rome
item.description: Lecture at Jackman Humanities Building, 170 St. George St.. Sulpicia: Life, Love, and Literature in Ancient Rome June 9, 2026 You're invited to celebrate the publication of Sulpicia: Life, Love, and Literature in Ancient Rome , by Alison Keith.
